Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call

NASA’s X-59 quiet supersonic demonstrator unexpectedly cut short its second test flight on March 20, 2026, after a return-to-base call. The… The post NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call appeared first on AeroTime.

NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic demonstrator encountered an unplanned early return during its second test flight, potentially impacting the critical sonic boom reduction validation timeline.

See AeroTime Hub →

Safety Mar 20, 2026

Delta Air Lines Grounds Airbus A319 After Cabin Loses Electrical Power Inflight

The flight made an emergency landing at Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport after the cabin temporarily lost power during its final approach.

Unplanned electrical failures during critical flight phases highlight the need for enhanced redundancy protocols and preflight diagnostics in aging aircraft systems.

See Simple Flying →

Business Aviation Mar 20, 2026

NBAA Study Examines Aviation Workforce Gaps, Safety Impact

New report highlights pilot demand in business aviation and operator responses.

Business aviation faces critical pilot shortages that directly threaten operational safety, requiring industry-wide recruitment and retention strategies.

See AVweb →

Military Mar 20, 2026

How The Eurofighter Typhoon Stacks Up Against The Sukhoi Su-35 In 2026

Both jets sit at the top tier of fourth-generation fighters, sometimes referred to as 'generation 4.5'.

Fourth-generation fighter superiority in 2026 will hinge on avionics, sensor fusion, and pilot training rather than raw aircraft performance metrics.

Drones Mar 20, 2026

DJI Flip now cheaper than many beginner drones

There’s a reason drones often sit in online carts longer than they should. It’s not just about cost; it’s the friction. Too many controls. Too much setup. Too many things that can go wrong in the first five minutes. DJI Flip was designed to chip away at that hesitation. And now, with a fresh roun...

DJI Flip's aggressive pricing and simplified user experience reduce barriers to drone adoption by addressing beginner hesitation and operational complexity.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition

NASA selected 14 university teams from across the nation as finalists in the 2026 Revolutionary Aerospace Systems Concepts – Academic Linkage (RASC-AL) Competition. This NASA challenge tasks students to design innovative concepts that could further human life and work on the Moon, Mars, and beyon...

NASA's RASC-AL competition is cultivating next-generation aerospace engineers who will develop critical technologies for lunar and Mars exploration missions.

See NASA →

Army Receives First Optionally Piloted Black Hawk

The helicopter is expected to support autonomy evaluations as the service advances modernization efforts.

Army's optionally piloted Black Hawk enables autonomous flight testing to accelerate helicopter modernization and operational capability advancement.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries

The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS will soon leave our solar system, never to return, but the observations of the comet will live on in NASA’s public data archives. More than a dozen NASA science missions turned their instruments to observe the comet, which is only the third identified object to be ...

NASA's open-source comet observation data demonstrates how public scientific archives accelerate aerospace research and discovery across multiple mission platforms.

Industry Mar 20, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight operations offers the most immediate pathway to accommodate growing air traffic while reducing environmental impact.

US Army signals end of traditional combat with drone shift

In a recent live-fire exercise at Fort Campbell, Kentucky, the 101st Airborne Division tested how drones can work side-by-side with soldiers in real combat scenarios. The tak...

Military adoption of combat drones signals evolving operational doctrine that will influence defense budgets and aerospace contractor development priorities.
